key_points: Ratios help compare quantities in mathematics and real life
Two ratios are proportional if their cross products are equal
Map scales are used to represent large real distances in small map measurements
Multi-term ratios compare three or more quantities
Pie charts display proportional data visually using circular sectors
Direct proportion means both quantities increase or decrease together
Inverse proportion means one quantity increases while the other decreases
examples: Map scale example: 2 cm on map represents 120 km
Division example: 90 divided in ratio 2 : 3 = 36 and 54
Pie chart example: 20% data corresponds to 72°
exam_tips: Always reduce ratios to their simplest form
When dividing quantities, first find total ratio parts
Identify whether the situation involves direct or inverse proportion
Write clear steps while solving ratio problems
